docs.title: Openbox Dotfiles
docs.text:
Welcome to my custom Openbox configuration! This repository contains a highly polished, lightweight, and plug-and-play setup for the Openbox stacking window manager.

Openbox is an extremely fast, lightweight, and highly configurable window manager for the X Window System. Unlike tiling window managers, Openbox is a stacking window manager, meaning windows can overlap and be freely resized, but it still offers powerful keyboard-driven workflows and deep customization through its XML-based configuration files.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Lightweight & Fast: Minimal resource footprint, perfect for older hardware or maximizing system performance.
  • Dual Conky Setup: Beautiful system monitoring on both the left and right sides of the screen.
  • Auzia-Conky Integration: Stunning Lua-based conky theme for the left panel.
  • Pre-configured Keybinds: Carefully mapped keyboard shortcuts in rc.xml for a seamless workflow.
  • Custom Wallpapers: A curated selection of Arch Linux-themed wallpapers included.

docs.title: Conky Setup
docs.text: This configuration features a beautiful dual conky setup that displays system statistics on both the left and right sides of your screen.

docs.title: Left Panel: Auzia-Conky
docs.text: The left panel uses the stunning auzia-conky theme, which is a Lua-based conky configuration featuring dynamic gauges and a modern aesthetic.

docs.title: Important Requirement
docs.text:
To get auzia-conky working properly, you must have conky-lua-nv installed from the AUR. The standard conky package from the official repositories does not support the required Lua NV bindings.

Install it with:

```bash trizen -S conky-lua-nv

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Follow these steps to install and set up this Openbox configuration on your Arch Linux system.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Ensure you have the following packages installed: ```bash sudo pacman -S openbox xfce-power-manager tint2 xorg-xinit pcmanfm lxterminal nwg-look

Packages installed with trizen (AUR helper) conky-lua-nv obconf-qt obmenu-generator openbox-themes flat-remix-gtk Note: conky-lua-nv is strictly required to get the auzia-conky theme working properly Clone or download this repository. Copy the configuration files to your ~/.config/ directory: cp -r configs/openbox/* ~/.config/openbox/

docs.title: Theming
docs.text: Openbox is themed using standard Openbox themes, which control window borders, title bars, and buttons.

docs.title: Configuration Files
docs.text:
• rc.xml: The main configuration file. Look for the <theme> section to change your Openbox theme.
  • menu.xml: Defines the right-click desktop menu and application categories.

docs.title: GTK Theme
docs.text:
This setup uses the Flat Remix GTK theme for a modern, clean appearance. You can change the GTK theme using nwg-look:

```bash nwg-look
